revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 庭田神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Niwata Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px|]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[兵庫県]][[宍粟市]]一宮町能倉1286 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 06|緯度秒 = 48 |経度度 = 134|経度分 = 36|経度秒 = 28 |祭神 = [[事代主命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[村社]] |創建 = [[成務天皇]]14年（[[131年]]）[[2月11日]] |本殿 = [[入母屋造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月18日]] |神事 = |地図 = Japan Hyogo }} [[File:Niwata Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''庭田神社'''（にわたじんじゃ）は、[[兵庫県]][[宍粟市]]にある神社。[[式内社|式内小社]]で、[[旧社格]]は村社。 == 歴史 == === 創建 === 社伝によると、[[成務天皇]]14年（[[131年]]）[[2月11日]]に神託により神祠を建てたことが創祀とされ、[[神功皇后]]期に本殿や諸殿が造営されたという。 『[[播磨国風土記]]』によると、伊和大神（[[大己貴神]]）が伊和の地で最後の交渉を終え、国造りの大業の達成に力を合わせた諸神々を集め、酒を醸し山河の清庭の地を選び慰労の饗宴を開いたといい、当社は饗宴を開いた山河の清庭であるという。この時非常食として持参した干飯を水で戻した時に一部の飯にカビが生え、酒成分が生じて酒の発見になったことが記されている。 === 概史 === [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では[[播磨国]][[宍粟郡]]に「庭田神社」と記載され、[[式内社|式内小社]]に列している。 [[寛文]]11年（[[1671年]]）に社殿を修善したが、[[文久]]3年（[[1863年]]）11月に焼失し、[[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）に再建された。[[明治]]7年（[[1873年]]）2月に[[村社]]に列格した。 == 境内 == File:Niwata Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 File:Niwata Jinja Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 File:Niwata Jinja Massha.jpg|末社 File:Niwata Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|神楽殿 File:Niwata Jinja Kameishi.jpg|亀石 == 現地情報 == === 所在地 === *[[兵庫県]][[宍粟市]]一宮町能倉1286 === 周辺 === *[[伊和神社]]…南西へ3km == 関連項目 == *[[播磨国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6320091.html 庭田神社]（兵庫県神社庁） {{神道 横}}{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にわたしんしや}} [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]]

== Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Shisō District, Hyōgo|en|Shisō District, Hyōgo|ja|宍粟郡|fa|ناحیه شیسو، هیوگو|zh|宍粟郡|lt=Shisō district|WD=Q7499122}} || {{ill|Niwata Shrine|lt=Niwata Shrine|ja|庭田神社|commons|Category:Niwata-jinja|WD=Q87433102}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|35.113609|134.608049|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183030 DB] |}

{{出典の明記|date=2020年3月}} {{神社 |名称 = 庭田神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Niwata Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[兵庫県]][[宍粟市]]一宮町能倉1286 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[事代主命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[村社]] |創建 = [[成務天皇]]14年（[[131年]]）[[2月11日]] |本殿 = [[入母屋造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月18日]] |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=庭田神社|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Niwata Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''庭田神社'''（にわたじんじゃ）は、[[兵庫県]][[宍粟市]]にある神社。[[式内社|式内小社]]で、[[旧社格]]は村社。 == 歴史 == === 創建 === 社伝によると、[[成務天皇]]14年（[[131年]]）[[2月11日]]に神託により神祠を建てたことが創祀とされ、[[神功皇后]]期に本殿や諸殿が造営されたという。 『[[播磨国風土記]]』によると、伊和大神（[[大己貴神]]）が伊和の地で最後の交渉を終え、国造りの大業の達成に力を合わせた諸神々を集め、酒を醸し山河の清庭の地を選び慰労の饗宴を開いたといい、当社は饗宴を開いた山河の清庭であるという。この時非常食として持参した干飯を水で戻した時に一部の飯にカビが生え、酒成分が生じて酒の発見になったことが記されている。 === 概史 === [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では[[播磨国]][[宍粟郡]]に「庭田神社」と記載され、[[式内社|式内小社]]に列している。 [[寛文]]11年（[[1671年]]）に社殿を修善したが、[[文久]]3年（[[1863年]]）11月に焼失し、[[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）に再建された。[[明治]]7年（[[1873年]]）2月に[[村社]]に列格した。 == 境内 == File:Niwata Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 File:Niwata Jinja Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 File:Niwata Jinja Massha.jpg|末社 File:Niwata Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|神楽殿 File:Niwata Jinja Kameishi.jpg|亀石 == 現地情報 == === 所在地 === *[[兵庫県]][[宍粟市]]一宮町能倉1286 === 周辺 === *[[伊和神社]]…南西へ3km == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[播磨国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6320091.html 庭田神社]（兵庫県神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にわたしんしや}} [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]]

{{nihongo|'''Niwata Shrine'''|庭田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Niwata Shrine'''|庭田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Niwata Shrine'''|庭田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Niwata Shrine'''|庭田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Niwata Shrine'''|庭田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Niwata Shrine'''|庭田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Niwata Shrine'''|庭田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Niwata Shrine'''|庭田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].

{{Infobox religious building | name = Niwata Shrine | native_name = 庭田神社 | image = Niwata Jinja Haiden.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Niwata Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.113609|134.608049}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Kotoshironushi|lt=Kotoshironushi|en|Kotoshironushi|fa|کوتوشیرونوشی|fr|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主|ko|고토시로누시|ru|Котосиронуси|simple|Kotoshironushi|th|โคโตชิโรนูชิ|zh|事代主神|WD=Q8009611}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Niwata Shrine'''|庭田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Niwata Shrine'''|庭田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]].

revision.comment: 7 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2020年3月}} {{神社 |名称 = 庭田神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Niwata Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[兵庫県]][[宍粟市]]一宮町能倉1286 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[事代主命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[村社]] |創建 = [[成務天皇]]14年（[[131年]]）[[2月11日]] |本殿 = [[入母屋造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月18日]] |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=庭田神社|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Niwata Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''庭田神社'''（にわたじんじゃ）は、[[兵庫県]][[宍粟市]]にある神社。[[式内社|式内小社]]で、[[旧社格]]は村社。 == 歴史 == === 創建 === 社伝によると、[[成務天皇]]14年（[[131年]]）[[2月11日]]に神託により神祠を建てたことが創祀とされ、[[神功皇后]]期に本殿や諸殿が造営されたという。 『[[播磨国風土記]]』によると、伊和大神（[[大己貴神]]）が伊和の地で最後の交渉を終え、国造りの大業の達成に力を合わせた諸神々を集め、酒を醸し山河の清庭の地を選び慰労の饗宴を開いたといい、当社は饗宴を開いた山河の清庭であるという。この時非常食として持参した干飯を水で戻した時に一部の飯にカビが生え、酒成分が生じて酒の発見になったことが記されている。 === 概史 === [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では[[播磨国]][[宍粟郡]]に「庭田神社」と記載され、[[式内社|式内小社]]に列している。 [[寛文]]11年（[[1671年]]）に社殿を修善したが、[[文久]]3年（[[1863年]]）11月に焼失し、[[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）に再建された。[[明治]]7年（[[1873年]]）2月に[[村社]]に列格した。 == 境内 == File:Niwata Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 File:Niwata Jinja Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 File:Niwata Jinja Massha.jpg|末社 File:Niwata Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|神楽殿 File:Niwata Jinja Kameishi.jpg|亀石 == 現地情報 == === 所在地 === *[[兵庫県]][[宍粟市]]一宮町能倉1286 === 周辺 === *[[伊和神社]]…南西へ3km == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[播磨国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6320091.html 庭田神社]（兵庫県神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にわたしんしや}} [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]]

page|ja|庭田神社|version=107344749|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{出典の明記|date=2020年3月}} {{神社 |名称 = 庭田神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Niwata Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[兵庫県]][[宍粟市]]一宮町能倉1286 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[事代主命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[村社]] |創建 = [[成務天皇]]14年（[[131年]]）[[2月11日]] |本殿 = [[入母屋造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月18日]] |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=庭田神社|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Niwata Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''庭田神社'''（にわたじんじゃ）は、[[兵庫県]][[宍粟市]]にある神社。[[式内社|式内小社]]で、[[旧社格]]は村社。 == 歴史 == === 創建 === 社伝によると、[[成務天皇]]14年（[[131年]]）[[2月11日]]に神託により神祠を建てたことが創祀とされ、[[神功皇后]]期に本殿や諸殿が造営されたという。 『[[播磨国風土記]]』によると、伊和大神（[[大己貴神]]）が伊和の地で最後の交渉を終え、国造りの大業の達成に力を合わせた諸神々を集め、酒を醸し山河の清庭の地を選び慰労の饗宴を開いたといい、当社は饗宴を開いた山河の清庭であるという。この時非常食として持参した干飯を水で戻した時に一部の飯にカビが生え、酒成分が生じて酒の発見になったことが記されている。 === 概史 === [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では[[播磨国]][[宍粟郡]]に「庭田神社」と記載され、[[式内社|式内小社]]に列している。 [[寛文]]11年（[[1671年]]）に社殿を修善したが、[[文久]]3年（[[1863年]]）11月に焼失し、[[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）に再建された。[[明治]]7年（[[1873年]]）2月に[[村社]]に列格した。 == 境内 == File:Niwata Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 File:Niwata Jinja Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 File:Niwata Jinja Massha.jpg|末社 File:Niwata Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|神楽殿 File:Niwata Jinja Kameishi.jpg|亀石 == 現地情報 == === 所在地 === *[[兵庫県]][[宍粟市]]一宮町能倉1286 === 周辺 === *[[伊和神社]]…南西へ3km == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[播磨国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6320091.html 庭田神社]（兵庫県神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にわたしんしや}} [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]] ==End Japanese==

旧[[村社]] |創建 = [[成務天皇]]14年（[[131年]]）[[2月11日]] |本殿 = [[入母屋造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月18日]] |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=庭田神社|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Niwata Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''庭田神社'''（にわたじんじゃ）は、[[兵庫県]][[宍粟市]]にある神社。[[式内社|式内小社]]で、[[旧社格]]は村社。 == 歴史 == === 創建 === 社伝によると、[[成務天皇]]14年（[[131年]]）[[2月11日]]に神託により神祠を建てたことが創祀とされ、[[神功皇后]]期に本殿や諸殿が造営されたという。 『[[播磨国風土記]]』によると、伊和大神（[[大己貴神]]）が伊和の地で最後の交渉を終え、国造りの大業の達成に力を合わせた諸神々を集め、酒を醸し山河の清庭の地を選び慰労の饗宴を開いたといい、当社は饗宴を開いた山河の清庭であるという。この時非常食として持参した干飯を水で戻した時に一部の飯にカビが生え、酒成分が生じて酒の発見になったことが記されている。 === 概史 === [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では[[播磨国]][[宍粟郡]]に「庭田神社」と記載され、[[式内社|式内小社]]に列している。 [[寛文]]11年（[[1671年]]）に社殿を修善したが、[[文久]]3年（[[1863年]]）11月に焼失し、[[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）に再建された。[[明治]]7年（[[1873年]]）2月に[[村社]]に列格した。 == 境内 == File:Niwata Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 File:Niwata Jinja Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 File:Niwata Jinja Massha.jpg|末社 File:Niwata Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|神楽殿 File:Niwata Jinja Kameishi.jpg|亀石 == 現地情報 == === 所在地 === *[[兵庫県]][[宍粟市]]一宮町能倉1286 === 周辺 === *[[伊和神社]]…南西へ3km == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[播磨国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6320091.html 庭田神社]（兵庫県神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]] ==End Japanese==

旧[[村社]] |創建 = [[成務天皇]]14年（[[131年]]）[[2月11日]] |本殿 = [[入母屋造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月18日]] |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=庭田神社|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Niwata Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''庭田神社'''（にわたじんじゃ）は、[[兵庫県]][[宍粟市]]にある神社。[[式内社|式内小社]]で、[[旧社格]]は村社。 == 歴史 == === 創建 === 社伝によると、[[成務天皇]]14年（[[131年]]）[[2月11日]]に神託により神祠を建てたことが創祀とされ、[[神功皇后]]期に本殿や諸殿が造営されたという。 『[[播磨国風土記]]』によると、伊和大神（[[大己貴神]]）が伊和の地で最後の交渉を終え、国造りの大業の達成に力を合わせた諸神々を集め、酒を醸し山河の清庭の地を選び慰労の饗宴を開いたといい、当社は饗宴を開いた山河の清庭であるという。この時非常食として持参した干飯を水で戻した時に一部の飯にカビが生え、酒成分が生じて酒の発見になったことが記されている。 === 概史 === [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では[[播磨国]][[宍粟郡]]に「庭田神社」と記載され、[[式内社|式内小社]]に列している。 [[寛文]]11年（[[1671年]]）に社殿を修善したが、[[文久]]3年（[[1863年]]）11月に焼失し、[[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）に再建された。[[明治]]7年（[[1873年]]）2月に[[村社]]に列格した。 == 境内 == File:Niwata Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 File:Niwata Jinja Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 File:Niwata Jinja Massha.jpg|末社 File:Niwata Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|神楽殿 File:Niwata Jinja Kameishi.jpg|亀石 == 現地情報 == === 所在地 === *[[兵庫県]][[宍粟市]]一宮町能倉1286 === 周辺 === *[[伊和神社]]…南西へ3km == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[播磨国の式内社一覧]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6320091.html 庭田神社]（兵庫県神社庁） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]]

'''庭田神社'''（にわたじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|宍粟市|Q877216}}にある神社。{{jalink|式内社||式内小社}}で、{{jalink|旧社格}}は村社。 == 歴史 == === 創建 === 社伝によると、{{jalink|成務天皇|Q316672}}14年（{{jalink|131年|Q31740}}）{{jalink|2月11日|Q2335}}に神託により神祠を建てたことが創祀とされ、{{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803}}期に本殿や諸殿が造営されたという。 『{{jalink|播磨国風土記|Q11498726}}』によると、伊和大神（{{jalink|大己貴神}}）が伊和の地で最後の交渉を終え、国造りの大業の達成に力を合わせた諸神々を集め、酒を醸し山河の清庭の地を選び慰労の饗宴を開いたといい、当社は饗宴を開いた山河の清庭であるという。この時非常食として持参した干飯を水で戻した時に一部の飯にカビが生え、酒成分が生じて酒の発見になったことが記されている。 [[ファイル:Niwata Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|宍粟市|Q877216}}一宮町能倉1286 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|事代主命}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = {{jalink|成務天皇|Q316672}}14年（{{jalink|131年|Q31740}}）{{jalink|2月11日|Q2335}} |本殿 = {{jalink|入母屋造|Q855025}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月18日|Q2964}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=庭田神社|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Niwata Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''庭田神社'''（にわたじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|宍粟市|Q877216}}にある神社。{{jalink|式内社||式内小社}}で、{{jalink|旧社格}}は村社。 === 歴史 === === 創建 === 社伝によると、{{jalink|成務天皇|Q316672}}14年（{{jalink|131年|Q31740}}）{{jalink|2月11日|Q2335}}に神託により神祠を建てたことが創祀とされ、{{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803}}期に本殿や諸殿が造営されたという。 『{{jalink|播磨国風土記|Q11498726}}』によると、伊和大神（{{jalink|大己貴神}}）が伊和の地で最後の交渉を終え、国造りの大業の達成に力を合わせた諸神々を集め、酒を醸し山河の清庭の地を選び慰労の饗宴を開いたといい、当社は饗宴を開いた山河の清庭であるという。この時非常食として持参した干飯を水で戻した時に一部の飯にカビが生え、酒成分が生じて酒の発見になったことが記されている。 === 概史 === {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では{{jalink|播磨国|Q1047523}}{{jalink|宍粟郡|Q7499122}}に「庭田神社」と記載され、{{jalink|式内社||式内小社}}に列している。 {{jalink|寛文|Q1146228}}11年（{{jalink|1671年|Q7610}}）に社殿を修善したが、{{jalink|文久|Q730266}}3年（{{jalink|1863年|Q7701}}）11月に焼失し、{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}4年（{{jalink|1871年|Q7746}}）に再建された。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}7年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）2月に{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}に列格した。 === 境内 === File:Niwata Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 File:Niwata Jinja Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 File:Niwata Jinja Massha.jpg|末社 File:Niwata Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|神楽殿 File:Niwata Jinja Kameishi.jpg|亀石 === 現地情報 === === 所在地 === *{{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|宍粟市|Q877216}}一宮町能倉1286 === 周辺 === *{{jalink|伊和神社|Q65320}}…南西へ3km === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|播磨国の式内社一覧|Q11498723}} === 外部リンク === map|Japan Hyogo|label=庭田神社|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Niwata Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''庭田神社'''（にわたじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|宍粟市|Q877216}}にある神社。{{jalink|式内社||式内小社}}で、{{jalink|旧社格}}は村社。 === 歴史 === === 創建 === 社伝によると、{{jalink|成務天皇|Q316672}}14年（{{jalink|131年|Q31740}}）{{jalink|2月11日|Q2335}}に神託により神祠を建てたことが創祀とされ、{{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803}}期に本殿や諸殿が造営されたという。 『{{jalink|播磨国風土記|Q11498726}}』によると、伊和大神（{{jalink|大己貴神}}）が伊和の地で最後の交渉を終え、国造りの大業の達成に力を合わせた諸神々を集め、酒を醸し山河の清庭の地を選び慰労の饗宴を開いたといい、当社は饗宴を開いた山河の清庭であるという。この時非常食として持参した干飯を水で戻した時に一部の飯にカビが生え、酒成分が生じて酒の発見になったことが記されている。 === 概史 === {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では{{jalink|播磨国|Q1047523}}{{jalink|宍粟郡|Q7499122}}に「庭田神社」と記載され、{{jalink|式内社||式内小社}}に列している。 {{jalink|寛文|Q1146228}}11年（{{jalink|1671年|Q7610}}）に社殿を修善したが、{{jalink|文久|Q730266}}3年（{{jalink|1863年|Q7701}}）11月に焼失し、{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}4年（{{jalink|1871年|Q7746}}）に再建された。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}7年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）2月に{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}に列格した。 === 境内 === File:Niwata Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 File:Niwata Jinja Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 File:Niwata Jinja Massha.jpg|末社 File:Niwata Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|神楽殿 File:Niwata Jinja Kameishi.jpg|亀石 === 現地情報 === === 所在地 === *{{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|宍粟市|Q877216}}一宮町能倉1286 === 周辺 === *{{jalink|伊和神社|Q65320}}…南西へ3km === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|播磨国の式内社一覧|Q11498723}} === 外部リンク === [[ファイル:Niwata Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|宍粟市|Q877216}}一宮町能倉1286 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|事代主命}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = {{jalink|成務天皇|Q316672}}14年（{{jalink|131年|Q31740}}）{{jalink|2月11日|Q2335}} |本殿 = {{jalink|入母屋造|Q855025}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月18日|Q2964}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=庭田神社|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Niwata Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''庭田神社'''（にわたじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|宍粟市|Q877216}}にある神社。{{jalink|式内社||式内小社}}で、{{jalink|旧社格}}は村社。 === 歴史 === === 創建 === 社伝によると、{{jalink|成務天皇|Q316672}}14年（{{jalink|131年|Q31740}}）{{jalink|2月11日|Q2335}}に神託により神祠を建てたことが創祀とされ、{{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803}}期に本殿や諸殿が造営されたという。 『{{jalink|播磨国風土記|Q11498726}}』によると、伊和大神（{{jalink|大己貴神}}）が伊和の地で最後の交渉を終え、国造りの大業の達成に力を合わせた諸神々を集め、酒を醸し山河の清庭の地を選び慰労の饗宴を開いたといい、当社は饗宴を開いた山河の清庭であるという。この時非常食として持参した干飯を水で戻した時に一部の飯にカビが生え、酒成分が生じて酒の発見になったことが記されている。 === 概史 === {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では{{jalink|播磨国|Q1047523}}{{jalink|宍粟郡|Q7499122}}に「庭田神社」と記載され、{{jalink|式内社||式内小社}}に列している。 {{jalink|寛文|Q1146228}}11年（{{jalink|1671年|Q7610}}）に社殿を修善したが、{{jalink|文久|Q730266}}3年（{{jalink|1863年|Q7701}}）11月に焼失し、{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}4年（{{jalink|1871年|Q7746}}）に再建された。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}7年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）2月に{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}に列格した。 === 境内 === File:Niwata Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 File:Niwata Jinja Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 File:Niwata Jinja Massha.jpg|末社 File:Niwata Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|神楽殿 File:Niwata Jinja Kameishi.jpg|亀石 === 現地情報 === === 所在地 === *{{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|宍粟市|Q877216}}一宮町能倉1286 === 周辺 === *{{jalink|伊和神社|Q65320}}…南西へ3km === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|播磨国の式内社一覧|Q11498723}} === 外部リンク === Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]] [[ファイル:Niwata Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|宍粟市|Q877216}}一宮町能倉1286 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|事代主命}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = {{jalink|成務天皇|Q316672}}14年（{{jalink|131年|Q31740}}）{{jalink|2月11日|Q2335}} |本殿 = {{jalink|入母屋造|Q855025}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月18日|Q2964}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=庭田神社|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Niwata Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''庭田神社'''（にわたじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|宍粟市|Q877216}}にある神社。{{jalink|式内社||式内小社}}で、{{jalink|旧社格}}は村社。 === 歴史 === === 創建 === 社伝によると、{{jalink|成務天皇|Q316672}}14年（{{jalink|131年|Q31740}}）{{jalink|2月11日|Q2335}}に神託により神祠を建てたことが創祀とされ、{{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803}}期に本殿や諸殿が造営されたという。 『{{jalink|播磨国風土記|Q11498726}}』によると、伊和大神（{{jalink|大己貴神}}）が伊和の地で最後の交渉を終え、国造りの大業の達成に力を合わせた諸神々を集め、酒を醸し山河の清庭の地を選び慰労の饗宴を開いたといい、当社は饗宴を開いた山河の清庭であるという。この時非常食として持参した干飯を水で戻した時に一部の飯にカビが生え、酒成分が生じて酒の発見になったことが記されている。 === 概史 === {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では{{jalink|播磨国|Q1047523}}{{jalink|宍粟郡|Q7499122}}に「庭田神社」と記載され、{{jalink|式内社||式内小社}}に列している。 {{jalink|寛文|Q1146228}}11年（{{jalink|1671年|Q7610}}）に社殿を修善したが、{{jalink|文久|Q730266}}3年（{{jalink|1863年|Q7701}}）11月に焼失し、{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}4年（{{jalink|1871年|Q7746}}）に再建された。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}7年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）2月に{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}に列格した。 === 境内 === File:Niwata Jinja Honden.jpg|本殿 File:Niwata Jinja Zuishinmon.jpg|随神門 File:Niwata Jinja Massha.jpg|末社 File:Niwata Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|神楽殿 File:Niwata Jinja Kameishi.jpg|亀石 === 現地情報 === === 所在地 === *{{jalink|兵庫県|Q130290}}{{jalink|宍粟市|Q877216}}一宮町能倉1286 === 周辺 === *{{jalink|伊和神社|Q65320}}…南西へ3km === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|播磨国の式内社一覧|Q11498723}} === 外部リンク === Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] Niwata Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Niwata Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}{{jalink|Shisō|Q877216}}Ichinomiya-chō Nōkura 1286 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Year 14 of {{jalink|Emperor Seimu|Q316672}} ({{jalink|131|Q31740}}) {{jalink|February 11|Q2335}} |本殿 = {{jalink|irimoya-zukuri|Q855025}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 18|Q2964}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Niwata Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Niwata Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Niwata Shrine''' (にわたじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{jalink|Shisō|Q877216}}, {{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha||small Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{jalink|old shrine rank}} was village shrine. === History === === Founding === According to shrine tradition, its beginning was the construction of a sacred sanctuary following divine revelation on {{jalink|February 11|Q2335}}, year 14 of {{jalink|Emperor Seimu|Q316672}} ({{jalink|131|Q31740}}), and it is said that the honden and other buildings were later built in the time of {{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803}}. According to the '{{jalink|Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726}}', after Iwa Ōkami ({{jalink|Ōnamuchi-no-Kami}}) completed final negotiations in the land of Iwa, he gathered the deities who had joined in achieving the great work of nation-building, brewed sake, selected a pure court of mountains and rivers, and held a banquet of consolation. This shrine is said to be that pure court where the banquet was held. It also records that when dried rice carried as emergency food was rehydrated, part of it molded and produced alcohol components, leading to the discovery of sake. === Historical outline === In the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|register of deities}}, completed in year 5 of {{jalink|Enchō (era)|Q1327274|Enchō}} ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), "Niwata Shrine" is listed in {{jalink|Shisō District|Q7499122}} of {{jalink|Harima Province|Q1047523}}, and ranked as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha||small Shikinaisha}}. The shrine buildings were repaired in year 11 of {{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} ({{jalink|1671|Q7610}}), but burned down in November of year 3 of {{jalink|Bunkyū|Q730266}} ({{jalink|1863|Q7701}}), and were rebuilt in year 4 of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} ({{jalink|1871|Q7746}}). In February of Meiji 7 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was ranked as a {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. === Precinct === File:Niwata Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Niwata Jinja Zuishinmon.jpg|Zuishin gate File:Niwata Jinja Massha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine File:Niwata Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Niwata Jinja Kameishi.jpg|Kame-ishi (turtle stone) === Local information === === Location === *{{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}{{jalink|Shisō|Q877216}}Ichinomiya-chō Nōkura 1286 === Nearby === *{{jalink|Iwa Shrine|Q65320}} ... 3 km to the southwest === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Harima Province|Q11498723}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6320091.html Niwata Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}{{jalink|Shisō|Q877216}}Ichinomiya-chō Nōkura 1286 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Year 14 of {{jalink|Emperor Seimu|Q316672}} ({{jalink|131|Q31740}}) {{jalink|February 11|Q2335}} |本殿 = {{jalink|irimoya-zukuri|Q855025}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 18|Q2964}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Niwata Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Niwata Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Niwata Shrine''' (にわたじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{jalink|Shisō|Q877216}}, {{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha||small Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{jalink|old shrine rank}} was village shrine. === History === === Founding === According to shrine tradition, its beginning was the construction of a sacred sanctuary following divine revelation on {{jalink|February 11|Q2335}}, year 14 of {{jalink|Emperor Seimu|Q316672}} ({{jalink|131|Q31740}}), and it is said that the honden and other buildings were later built in the time of {{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803}}. According to the '{{jalink|Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726}}', after Iwa Ōkami ({{jalink|Ōnamuchi-no-Kami}}) completed final negotiations in the land of Iwa, he gathered the deities who had joined in achieving the great work of nation-building, brewed sake, selected a pure court of mountains and rivers, and held a banquet of consolation. This shrine is said to be that pure court where the banquet was held. It also records that when dried rice carried as emergency food was rehydrated, part of it molded and produced alcohol components, leading to the discovery of sake. === Historical outline === In the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|register of deities}}, completed in year 5 of {{jalink|Enchō (era)|Q1327274|Enchō}} ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), "Niwata Shrine" is listed in {{jalink|Shisō District|Q7499122}} of {{jalink|Harima Province|Q1047523}}, and ranked as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha||small Shikinaisha}}. The shrine buildings were repaired in year 11 of {{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} ({{jalink|1671|Q7610}}), but burned down in November of year 3 of {{jalink|Bunkyū|Q730266}} ({{jalink|1863|Q7701}}), and were rebuilt in year 4 of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} ({{jalink|1871|Q7746}}). In February of Meiji 7 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was ranked as a {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. === Precinct === File:Niwata Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Niwata Jinja Zuishinmon.jpg|Zuishin gate File:Niwata Jinja Massha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine File:Niwata Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Niwata Jinja Kameishi.jpg|Kame-ishi (turtle stone) === Local information === === Location === *{{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}{{jalink|Shisō|Q877216}}Ichinomiya-chō Nōkura 1286 === Nearby === *{{jalink|Iwa Shrine|Q65320}} ... 3 km to the southwest === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Harima Province|Q11498723}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6320091.html Niwata Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

Niwata Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Niwata Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}{{jalink|Shisō|Q877216}}Ichinomiya-chō Nōkura 1286 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Year 14 of {{jalink|Emperor Seimu|Q316672}} ({{jalink|131|Q31740}}) {{jalink|February 11|Q2335}} |本殿 = {{jalink|irimoya-zukuri|Q855025}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 18|Q2964}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Niwata Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Niwata Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Niwata Shrine''' (にわたじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{jalink|Shisō|Q877216}}, {{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha||small Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{jalink|old shrine rank}} was village shrine. === History === === Founding === According to shrine tradition, its beginning was the construction of a sacred sanctuary following divine revelation on {{jalink|February 11|Q2335}}, year 14 of {{jalink|Emperor Seimu|Q316672}} ({{jalink|131|Q31740}}), and it is said that the honden and other buildings were later built in the time of {{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803}}. According to the '{{jalink|Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726}}', after Iwa Ōkami ({{jalink|Ōnamuchi-no-Kami}}) completed final negotiations in the land of Iwa, he gathered the deities who had joined in achieving the great work of nation-building, brewed sake, selected a pure court of mountains and rivers, and held a banquet of consolation. This shrine is said to be that pure court where the banquet was held. It also records that when dried rice carried as emergency food was rehydrated, part of it molded and produced alcohol components, leading to the discovery of sake. === Historical outline === In the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|register of deities}}, completed in year 5 of {{jalink|Enchō (era)|Q1327274|Enchō}} ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), "Niwata Shrine" is listed in {{jalink|Shisō District|Q7499122}} of {{jalink|Harima Province|Q1047523}}, and ranked as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha||small Shikinaisha}}. The shrine buildings were repaired in year 11 of {{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} ({{jalink|1671|Q7610}}), but burned down in November of year 3 of {{jalink|Bunkyū|Q730266}} ({{jalink|1863|Q7701}}), and were rebuilt in year 4 of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} ({{jalink|1871|Q7746}}). In February of Meiji 7 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was ranked as a {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. === Precinct === File:Niwata Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Niwata Jinja Zuishinmon.jpg|Zuishin gate File:Niwata Jinja Massha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine File:Niwata Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Niwata Jinja Kameishi.jpg|Kame-ishi (turtle stone) === Local information === === Location === *{{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}{{jalink|Shisō|Q877216}}Ichinomiya-chō Nōkura 1286 === Nearby === *{{jalink|Iwa Shrine|Q65320}} ... 3 km to the southwest === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Harima Province|Q11498723}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6320091.html Niwata Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

Niwata Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Niwata Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}{{jalink|Shisō|Q877216}}Ichinomiya-chō Nōkura 1286 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Year 14 of {{jalink|Emperor Seimu|Q316672}} ({{jalink|131|Q31740}}) {{jalink|February 11|Q2335}} |本殿 = {{jalink|irimoya-zukuri|Q855025}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 18|Q2964}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Niwata Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Niwata Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Niwata Shrine''' (にわたじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{jalink|Shisō|Q877216}}, {{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha||small Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{jalink|old shrine rank}} was village shrine. === History === === Founding === According to shrine tradition, its beginning was the construction of a sacred sanctuary following divine revelation on {{jalink|February 11|Q2335}}, year 14 of {{jalink|Emperor Seimu|Q316672}} ({{jalink|131|Q31740}}), and it is said that the honden and other buildings were later built in the time of {{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803}}. According to the '{{jalink|Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726}}', after Iwa Ōkami ({{jalink|Ōnamuchi-no-Kami}}) completed final negotiations in the land of Iwa, he gathered the deities who had joined in achieving the great work of nation-building, brewed sake, selected a pure court of mountains and rivers, and held a banquet of consolation. This shrine is said to be that pure court where the banquet was held. It also records that when dried rice carried as emergency food was rehydrated, part of it molded and produced alcohol components, leading to the discovery of sake. === Historical outline === In the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|register of deities}}, completed in year 5 of {{jalink|Enchō (era)|Q1327274|Enchō}} ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), "Niwata Shrine" is listed in {{jalink|Shisō District|Q7499122}} of {{jalink|Harima Province|Q1047523}}, and ranked as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha||small Shikinaisha}}. The shrine buildings were repaired in year 11 of {{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} ({{jalink|1671|Q7610}}), but burned down in November of year 3 of {{jalink|Bunkyū|Q730266}} ({{jalink|1863|Q7701}}), and were rebuilt in year 4 of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} ({{jalink|1871|Q7746}}). In February of Meiji 7 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was ranked as a {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. === Precinct === File:Niwata Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Niwata Jinja Zuishinmon.jpg|Zuishin gate File:Niwata Jinja Massha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine File:Niwata Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Niwata Jinja Kameishi.jpg|Kame-ishi (turtle stone) === Local information === === Location === *{{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}{{jalink|Shisō|Q877216}}Ichinomiya-chō Nōkura 1286 === Nearby === *{{jalink|Iwa Shrine|Q65320}} ... 3 km to the southwest === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Harima Province|Q11498723}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6320091.html Niwata Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]]

This shrine is said to be that pure court where the banquet was held. It also records that when dried rice carried as emergency food was rehydrated, part of it molded and produced alcohol components, leading to the discovery of sake. === Historical outline === In the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|register of deities}}, completed in year 5 of {{jalink|Enchō (era)|Q1327274|Enchō}} ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), "Niwata Shrine" is listed in {{jalink|Shisō District|Q7499122}} of {{jalink|Harima Province|Q1047523}}, and ranked as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha||small Shikinaisha}}. The shrine buildings were repaired in year 11 of {{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} ({{jalink|1671|Q7610}}), but burned down in November of year 3 of {{jalink|Bunkyū|Q730266}} ({{jalink|1863|Q7701}}), and were rebuilt in year 4 of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} ({{jalink|1871|Q7746}}). In February of Meiji 7 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was ranked as a {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. === Precinct === File:Niwata Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Niwata Jinja Zuishinmon.jpg|Zuishin gate File:Niwata Jinja Massha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine File:Niwata Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Niwata Jinja Kameishi.jpg|Kame-ishi (turtle stone) === Local information === === Location === *{{jalink|Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290}}{{jalink|Shisō|Q877216}}Ichinomiya-chō Nōkura 1286 === Nearby === *{{jalink|Iwa Shrine|Q65320}} ... 3 km to the southwest === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Harima Province|Q11498723}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6320091.html Niwata Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Niwata Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Niwata Jinja Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216}}Ichinomiya-chō Nōkura 1286 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Year 14 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672}} ({{ill|lt=131|Q31740|WD=Q31740}}) {{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 18|Q2964|WD=Q2964}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Niwata Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Niwata Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Niwata Shrine''' (にわたじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha||WD=|lt=small Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=old shrine rank|1=UNKNOWN}} was village shrine. === History === === Founding === According to shrine tradition, its beginning was the construction of a sacred sanctuary following divine revelation on {{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335}}, year 14 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672}} ({{ill|lt=131|Q31740|WD=Q31740}}), and it is said that the honden and other buildings were later built in the time of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}}. According to the '{{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726}}', after Iwa Ōkami ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}) completed final negotiations in the land of Iwa, he gathered the deities who had joined in achieving the great work of nation-building, brewed sake, selected a pure court of mountains and rivers, and held a banquet of consolation. This shrine is said to be that pure court where the banquet was held. It also records that when dried rice carried as emergency food was rehydrated, part of it molded and produced alcohol components, leading to the discovery of sake. === Historical outline === In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities}}, completed in year 5 of {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), "Niwata Shrine" is listed in {{ill|lt=Shisō District|Q7499122|WD=Q7499122}} of {{ill|lt=Harima Province|Q1047523|WD=Q1047523}}, and ranked as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha||WD=|lt=small Shikinaisha}}. The shrine buildings were repaired in year 11 of {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} ({{ill|lt=1671|Q7610|WD=Q7610}}), but burned down in November of year 3 of {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|WD=Q730266}} ({{ill|lt=1863|Q7701|WD=Q7701}}), and were rebuilt in year 4 of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746}}). In February of Meiji 7 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Precinct === File:Niwata Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Niwata Jinja Zuishinmon.jpg|Zuishin gate File:Niwata Jinja Massha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine File:Niwata Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Niwata Jinja Kameishi.jpg|Kame-ishi (turtle stone) === Local information === === Location === *{{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216}}Ichinomiya-chō Nōkura 1286 === Nearby === *{{ill|lt=Iwa Shrine|Q65320|WD=Q65320}} ... 3 km to the southwest === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Harima Province|Q11498723|WD=Q11498723}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6320091.html Niwata Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216}}Ichinomiya-chō Nōkura 1286 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Year 14 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672}} ({{ill|lt=131|Q31740|WD=Q31740}}) {{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 18|Q2964|WD=Q2964}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Niwata Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Niwata Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Niwata Shrine''' (にわたじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=small Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=old shrine rank|1=UNKNOWN}} was village shrine. === History === === Founding === According to shrine tradition, its beginning was the construction of a sacred sanctuary following divine revelation on {{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335}}, year 14 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672}} ({{ill|lt=131|Q31740|WD=Q31740}}), and it is said that the honden and other buildings were later built in the time of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}}. According to the '{{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726}}', after Iwa Ōkami ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}) completed final negotiations in the land of Iwa, he gathered the deities who had joined in achieving the great work of nation-building, brewed sake, selected a pure court of mountains and rivers, and held a banquet of consolation. This shrine is said to be that pure court where the banquet was held. It also records that when dried rice carried as emergency food was rehydrated, part of it molded and produced alcohol components, leading to the discovery of sake. === Historical outline === In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities}}, completed in year 5 of {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), "Niwata Shrine" is listed in {{ill|lt=Shisō District|Q7499122|WD=Q7499122}} of {{ill|lt=Harima Province|Q1047523|WD=Q1047523}}, and ranked as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=small Shikinaisha}}. The shrine buildings were repaired in year 11 of {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} ({{ill|lt=1671|Q7610|WD=Q7610}}), but burned down in November of year 3 of {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|WD=Q730266}} ({{ill|lt=1863|Q7701|WD=Q7701}}), and were rebuilt in year 4 of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746}}). In February of Meiji 7 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Precinct === File:Niwata Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Niwata Jinja Zuishinmon.jpg|Zuishin gate File:Niwata Jinja Massha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine File:Niwata Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Niwata Jinja Kameishi.jpg|Kame-ishi (turtle stone) === Local information === === Location === *{{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216}}Ichinomiya-chō Nōkura 1286 === Nearby === *{{ill|lt=Iwa Shrine|Q65320|WD=Q65320}} ... 3 km to the southwest === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Harima Province|Q11498723|WD=Q11498723}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6320091.html Niwata Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Year 14 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672}} ({{ill|lt=131|Q31740|WD=Q31740}}) {{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 18|Q2964|WD=Q2964}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Niwata Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Niwata Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Niwata Shrine''' (にわたじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=small Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=old shrine rank|1=UNKNOWN}} was village shrine. === History === === Founding === According to shrine tradition, its beginning was the construction of a sacred sanctuary following divine revelation on {{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335}}, year 14 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672}} ({{ill|lt=131|Q31740|WD=Q31740}}), and it is said that the honden and other buildings were later built in the time of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}}. According to the '{{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726}}', after Iwa Ōkami ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}) completed final negotiations in the land of Iwa, he gathered the deities who had joined in achieving the great work of nation-building, brewed sake, selected a pure court of mountains and rivers, and held a banquet of consolation. This shrine is said to be that pure court where the banquet was held. It also records that when dried rice carried as emergency food was rehydrated, part of it molded and produced alcohol components, leading to the discovery of sake. === Historical outline === In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities}}, completed in year 5 of {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), "Niwata Shrine" is listed in {{ill|lt=Shisō District|Q7499122|WD=Q7499122}} of {{ill|lt=Harima Province|Q1047523|WD=Q1047523}}, and ranked as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=small Shikinaisha}}. The shrine buildings were repaired in year 11 of {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} ({{ill|lt=1671|Q7610|WD=Q7610}}), but burned down in November of year 3 of {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|WD=Q730266}} ({{ill|lt=1863|Q7701|WD=Q7701}}), and were rebuilt in year 4 of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746}}). In February of Meiji 7 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Precinct === File:Niwata Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Niwata Jinja Zuishinmon.jpg|Zuishin gate File:Niwata Jinja Massha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine File:Niwata Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Niwata Jinja Kameishi.jpg|Kame-ishi (turtle stone) === Local information === === Location === *{{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216}}Ichinomiya-chō Nōkura 1286 === Nearby === *{{ill|lt=Iwa Shrine|Q65320|WD=Q65320}} ... 3 km to the southwest === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Harima Province|Q11498723|WD=Q11498723}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6320091.html Niwata Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It also records that when dried rice carried as emergency food was rehydrated, part of it molded and produced alcohol components, leading to the discovery of sake. === Historical outline === In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities}}, completed in year 5 of {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), "Niwata Shrine" is listed in {{ill|lt=Shisō District|Q7499122|WD=Q7499122}} of {{ill|lt=Harima Province|Q1047523|WD=Q1047523}}, and ranked as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=small Shikinaisha}}. The shrine buildings were repaired in year 11 of {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} ({{ill|lt=1671|Q7610|WD=Q7610}}), but burned down in November of year 3 of {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|WD=Q730266}} ({{ill|lt=1863|Q7701|WD=Q7701}}), and were rebuilt in year 4 of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746}}). In February of Meiji 7 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Precinct === File:Niwata Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Niwata Jinja Zuishinmon.jpg|Zuishin gate File:Niwata Jinja Massha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine File:Niwata Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Niwata Jinja Kameishi.jpg|Kame-ishi (turtle stone) === Local information === === Location === *{{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216}}Ichinomiya-chō Nōkura 1286 === Nearby === *{{ill|lt=Iwa Shrine|Q65320|WD=Q65320}} ... 3 km to the southwest === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Harima Province|Q11498723|WD=Q11498723}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6320091.html Niwata Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=small Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=old shrine rank|1=UNKNOWN}} was village shrine. === History === === Founding === According to shrine tradition, its beginning was the construction of a sacred sanctuary following divine revelation on {{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335|1=February 11}}, year 14 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}} ({{ill|lt=131|Q31740|WD=Q31740|1=AD 131}}), and it is said that the honden and other buildings were later built in the time of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}. According to the '{{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726|1=Harima no Kuni Fudoki}}', after Iwa Ōkami ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}) completed final negotiations in the land of Iwa, he gathered the deities who had joined in achieving the great work of nation-building, brewed sake, selected a pure court of mountains and rivers, and held a banquet of consolation. This shrine is said to be that pure court where the banquet was held. It also records that when dried rice carried as emergency food was rehydrated, part of it molded and produced alcohol components, leading to the discovery of sake. === Historical outline === In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, completed in year 5 of {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), "Niwata Shrine" is listed in {{ill|lt=Shisō District|Q7499122|WD=Q7499122|1=Shisō District, Hyōgo}} of {{ill|lt=Harima Province|Q1047523|WD=Q1047523|1=Harima Province}}, and ranked as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=small Shikinaisha}}. The shrine buildings were repaired in year 11 of {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} ({{ill|lt=1671|Q7610|WD=Q7610|1=1671}}), but burned down in November of year 3 of {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|WD=Q730266|1=Bunkyū}} ({{ill|lt=1863|Q7701|WD=Q7701|1=1863}}), and were rebuilt in year 4 of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}). In February of Meiji 7 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Precinct === File:Niwata Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Niwata Jinja Zuishinmon.jpg|Zuishin gate File:Niwata Jinja Massha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine File:Niwata Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Niwata Jinja Kameishi.jpg|Kame-ishi (turtle stone) === Local information === === Location === *{{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}Ichinomiya-chō Nōkura 1286 === Nearby === *{{ill|lt=Iwa Shrine|Q65320|WD=Q65320|1=Iwa Shrine (Matsumoto)}} ... 3 km to the southwest === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Harima Province|Q11498723|WD=Q11498723|1=List of Shikinaisha in Harima Province}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6320091.html Niwata Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It also records that when dried rice carried as emergency food was rehydrated, part of it molded and produced alcohol components, leading to the discovery of sake. === Historical outline === In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, completed in year 5 of {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), "Niwata Shrine" is listed in {{ill|lt=Shisō District|Q7499122|WD=Q7499122|1=Shisō District, Hyōgo}} of {{ill|lt=Harima Province|Q1047523|WD=Q1047523|1=Harima Province}}, and ranked as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=small Shikinaisha}}. The shrine buildings were repaired in year 11 of {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} ({{ill|lt=1671|Q7610|WD=Q7610|1=1671}}), but burned down in November of year 3 of {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|WD=Q730266|1=Bunkyū}} ({{ill|lt=1863|Q7701|WD=Q7701|1=1863}}), and were rebuilt in year 4 of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}). In February of Meiji 7 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Precinct === File:Niwata Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Niwata Jinja Zuishinmon.jpg|Zuishin gate File:Niwata Jinja Massha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine File:Niwata Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Niwata Jinja Kameishi.jpg|Kame-ishi (turtle stone) === Local information === === Location === *{{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}Ichinomiya-chō Nōkura 1286 === Nearby === *{{ill|lt=Iwa Shrine|Q65320|WD=Q65320|1=Iwa Shrine (Matsumoto)}} ... 3 km to the southwest === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Harima Province|Q11498723|WD=Q11498723|1=List of Shikinaisha in Harima Province}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6320091.html Niwata Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=small Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=old shrine rank|1=UNKNOWN}} was village shrine. === History === === Founding === According to shrine tradition, its beginning was the construction of a sacred sanctuary following divine revelation on {{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335|1=February 11}}, year 14 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}} ({{ill|lt=131|Q31740|WD=Q31740|1=AD 131}}), and it is said that the honden and other buildings were later built in the time of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}. According to the '{{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726|1=Harima no Kuni Fudoki}}', after Iwa Ōkami ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}) completed final negotiations in the land of Iwa, he gathered the deities who had joined in achieving the great work of nation-building, brewed sake, selected a pure court of mountains and rivers, and held a banquet of consolation. This shrine is said to be that pure court where the banquet was held. It also records that when dried rice carried as emergency food was rehydrated, part of it molded and produced alcohol components, leading to the discovery of sake. === Historical outline === In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, completed in year 5 of {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), "Niwata Shrine" is listed in {{ill|lt=Shisō District|Q7499122|WD=Q7499122|1=Shisō District, Hyōgo}} of {{ill|lt=Harima Province|Q1047523|WD=Q1047523|1=Harima Province}}, and ranked as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=small Shikinaisha}}. The shrine buildings were repaired in year 11 of {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} ({{ill|lt=1671|Q7610|WD=Q7610|1=1671}}), but burned down in November of year 3 of {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|WD=Q730266|1=Bunkyū}} ({{ill|lt=1863|Q7701|WD=Q7701|1=1863}}), and were rebuilt in year 4 of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}). In February of Meiji 7 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Precinct === File:Niwata Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Niwata Jinja Zuishinmon.jpg|Zuishin gate File:Niwata Jinja Massha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine File:Niwata Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Niwata Jinja Kameishi.jpg|Kame-ishi (turtle stone) === Local information === === Location === *{{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}Ichinomiya-chō Nōkura 1286 === Nearby === *{{ill|lt=Iwa Shrine|Q65320|WD=Q65320|1=Iwa Shrine (Matsumoto)}} ... 3 km to the southwest === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Harima Province|Q11498723|WD=Q11498723|1=List of Shikinaisha in Harima Province}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6320091.html Niwata Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にわたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

It also records that when dried rice carried as emergency food was rehydrated, part of it molded and produced alcohol components, leading to the discovery of sake. === Historical outline === In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, completed in year 5 of {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), "Niwata Shrine" is listed in {{ill|lt=Shisō District|Q7499122|WD=Q7499122|1=Shisō District, Hyōgo}} of {{ill|lt=Harima Province|Q1047523|WD=Q1047523|1=Harima Province}}, and ranked as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=small Shikinaisha}}. The shrine buildings were repaired in year 11 of {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} ({{ill|lt=1671|Q7610|WD=Q7610|1=1671}}), but burned down in November of year 3 of {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|WD=Q730266|1=Bunkyū}} ({{ill|lt=1863|Q7701|WD=Q7701|1=1863}}), and were rebuilt in year 4 of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}). In February of Meiji 7 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Precinct === File:Niwata Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Niwata Jinja Zuishinmon.jpg|Zuishin gate File:Niwata Jinja Massha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine File:Niwata Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Niwata Jinja Kameishi.jpg|Kame-ishi (turtle stone) === Local information === === Location === *{{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}Ichinomiya-chō Nōkura 1286 === Nearby === *{{ill|lt=Iwa Shrine|Q65320|WD=Q65320|1=Iwa Shrine (Matsumoto)}} ... 3 km to the southwest === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Harima Province|Q11498723|WD=Q11498723|1=List of Shikinaisha in Harima Province}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6320091.html Niwata Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=small Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=old shrine rank|1=UNKNOWN}} was village shrine. === History === === Founding === According to shrine tradition, its beginning was the construction of a sacred sanctuary following divine revelation on {{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335|1=February 11}}, year 14 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}} ({{ill|lt=131|Q31740|WD=Q31740|1=AD 131}}), and it is said that the honden and other buildings were later built in the time of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}. This shrine is said to be that pure court where the banquet was held. It also records that when dried rice carried as emergency food was rehydrated, part of it molded and produced alcohol components, leading to the discovery of sake. === Historical outline === In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, completed in year 5 of {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), "Niwata Shrine" is listed in {{ill|lt=Shisō District|Q7499122|WD=Q7499122|1=Shisō District, Hyōgo}} of {{ill|lt=Harima Province|Q1047523|WD=Q1047523|1=Harima Province}}, and ranked as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=small Shikinaisha}}. The shrine buildings were repaired in year 11 of {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} ({{ill|lt=1671|Q7610|WD=Q7610|1=1671}}), but burned down in November of year 3 of {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|WD=Q730266|1=Bunkyū}} ({{ill|lt=1863|Q7701|WD=Q7701|1=1863}}), and were rebuilt in year 4 of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}). In February of Meiji 7 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Precinct === File:Niwata Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Niwata Jinja Zuishinmon.jpg|Zuishin gate File:Niwata Jinja Massha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine File:Niwata Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Niwata Jinja Kameishi.jpg|Kame-ishi (turtle stone) === Local information === === Location === *{{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}Ichinomiya-chō Nōkura 1286 === Nearby === *{{ill|lt=Iwa Shrine|Q65320|WD=Q65320|1=Iwa Shrine (Matsumoto)}} ... 3 km to the southwest === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Harima Province|Q11498723|WD=Q11498723|1=List of Shikinaisha in Harima Province}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6320091.html Niwata Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

It also records that when dried rice carried as emergency food was rehydrated, part of it molded and produced alcohol components, leading to the discovery of sake. === Historical outline === In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, completed in year 5 of {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), "Niwata Shrine" is listed in {{ill|lt=Shisō District|Q7499122|WD=Q7499122|1=Shisō District, Hyōgo}} of {{ill|lt=Harima Province|Q1047523|WD=Q1047523|1=Harima Province}}, and ranked as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=small Shikinaisha}}. The shrine buildings were repaired in year 11 of {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} ({{ill|lt=1671|Q7610|WD=Q7610|1=1671}}), but burned down in November of year 3 of {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|WD=Q730266|1=Bunkyū}} ({{ill|lt=1863|Q7701|WD=Q7701|1=1863}}), and were rebuilt in year 4 of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}). In February of Meiji 7 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Precinct === File:Niwata Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Niwata Jinja Zuishinmon.jpg|Zuishin gate File:Niwata Jinja Massha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine File:Niwata Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Niwata Jinja Kameishi.jpg|Kame-ishi (turtle stone) === Local information === === Location === *{{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}Ichinomiya-chō Nōkura 1286 === Nearby === *{{ill|lt=Iwa Shrine|Q65320|WD=Q65320|1=Iwa Shrine (Matsumoto)}} ... 3 km to the southwest === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Harima Province|Q11498723|WD=Q11498723|1=List of Shikinaisha in Harima Province}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6320091.html Niwata Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=small Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=old shrine rank|1=UNKNOWN}} was village shrine. === History === === Founding === According to shrine tradition, its beginning was the construction of a sacred sanctuary following divine revelation on {{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335|1=February 11}}, year 14 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}} ({{ill|lt=131|Q31740|WD=Q31740|1=AD 131}}), and it is said that the honden and other buildings were later built in the time of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}. According to the '{{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726|1=Harima no Kuni Fudoki}}', after Iwa Ōkami ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}) completed final negotiations in the land of Iwa, he gathered the deities who had joined in achieving the great work of nation-building, brewed sake, selected a pure court of mountains and rivers, and held a banquet of consolation. This shrine is said to be that pure court where the banquet was held. It also records that when dried rice carried as emergency food was rehydrated, part of it molded and produced alcohol components, leading to the discovery of sake. === Historical outline === In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, completed in year 5 of {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), "Niwata Shrine" is listed in {{ill|lt=Shisō District|Q7499122|WD=Q7499122|1=Shisō District, Hyōgo}} of {{ill|lt=Harima Province|Q1047523|WD=Q1047523|1=Harima Province}}, and ranked as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=small Shikinaisha}}. The shrine buildings were repaired in year 11 of {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} ({{ill|lt=1671|Q7610|WD=Q7610|1=1671}}), but burned down in November of year 3 of {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|WD=Q730266|1=Bunkyū}} ({{ill|lt=1863|Q7701|WD=Q7701|1=1863}}), and were rebuilt in year 4 of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}). In February of Meiji 7 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Precinct === File:Niwata Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Niwata Jinja Zuishinmon.jpg|Zuishin gate File:Niwata Jinja Massha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine File:Niwata Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Niwata Jinja Kameishi.jpg|Kame-ishi (turtle stone) === Local information === === Location === *{{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}Ichinomiya-chō Nōkura 1286 === Nearby === *{{ill|lt=Iwa Shrine|Q65320|WD=Q65320|1=Iwa Shrine (Matsumoto)}} ... 3 km to the southwest === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Harima Province|Q11498723|WD=Q11498723|1=List of Shikinaisha in Harima Province}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6320091.html Niwata Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

It also records that when dried rice carried as emergency food was rehydrated, part of it molded and produced alcohol components, leading to the discovery of sake. === Historical outline === In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, completed in year 5 of {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), "Niwata Shrine" is listed in {{ill|lt=Shisō District|Q7499122|WD=Q7499122|1=Shisō District, Hyōgo}} of {{ill|lt=Harima Province|Q1047523|WD=Q1047523|1=Harima Province}}, and ranked as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=small Shikinaisha}}. The shrine buildings were repaired in year 11 of {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} ({{ill|lt=1671|Q7610|WD=Q7610|1=1671}}), but burned down in November of year 3 of {{ill|lt=Bunkyū|Q730266|WD=Q730266|1=Bunkyū}} ({{ill|lt=1863|Q7701|WD=Q7701|1=1863}}), and were rebuilt in year 4 of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}). In February of Meiji 7 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Precinct === File:Niwata Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden File:Niwata Jinja Zuishinmon.jpg|Zuishin gate File:Niwata Jinja Massha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine File:Niwata Jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Niwata Jinja Kameishi.jpg|Kame-ishi (turtle stone) === Local information === === Location === *{{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}Ichinomiya-chō Nōkura 1286 === Nearby === *{{ill|lt=Iwa Shrine|Q65320|WD=Q65320|1=Iwa Shrine (Matsumoto)}} ... 3 km to the southwest === Notes === {{Reflist}} === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Harima Province|Q11498723|WD=Q11498723|1=List of Shikinaisha in Harima Province}} === External links === *[http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6320091.html Niwata Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Niwata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]]

It also records that when dried rice carried as emergency food was rehydrated, part of it molded and produced alcohol components, leading to the discovery of sake. === Historical outline === In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, completed in year 5 of {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), "Niwata Shrine" is listed in {{ill|lt=Shisō District|Q7499122|WD=Q7499122|1=Shisō District, Hyōgo}} of {{ill|lt=Harima Province|Q1047523|WD=Q1047523|1=Harima Province}}, and ranked as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=small Shikinaisha}}.
